steps.title: Use the tangent-radius relationship
steps.content: A tangent line is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency. First find the slope from the center $(-2,3)$ to the tangent point $(4,-5)$, then use the negative reciprocal for line $t$.

steps.title: Find the slope of the radius to the tangent point
steps.content:
The radius connects the center $(-2, 3)$ to the tangent point $(4, -5)$:
$$m_{\text{radius}} = \frac{-5 - 3}{4 - (-2)} = \frac{-8}{6} = -\frac{4}{3}$$

steps.title: Get the tangent line's slope and equation
steps.content:
A tangent is perpendicular to the radius at the point of tangency, so the tangent's slope is the negative reciprocal:
$$m_{\text{tangent}} = \frac{3}{4}$$
Using point-slope form through $(4, -5)$:
$$y + 5 = \frac{3}{4}(x - 4) \;\Rightarrow\; y = \frac{3}{4}x - 8$$

steps.title: Test each choice in $y = \frac{3}{4}x - 8$
steps.content:
• A) $\left(0, rac{3}{4} ight)$: $ rac{3}{4}(0) - 8 = -8 e rac{3}{4}$. Eliminate.
  • B) $(3, 6)$: $ rac{3}{4}(3) - 8 = rac{9}{4} - 8 = - rac{23}{4} e 6$. Eliminate.
  • C) $(12, 1)$: $ rac{3}{4}(12) - 8 = 9 - 8 = 1$. Match.
  • D) $(10, 3)$: $ rac{3}{4}(10) - 8 = rac{30}{4} - 8 = - rac{1}{2} e 3$. Eliminate.
That confirms C is correct.
